title: Saudi Cybersecurity Operations Center — Arabic Threat Intelligence
entry_mechanism: NCA-GADD joint programme building Arabic threat intelligence capability on top of existing Saudi SOC infrastructure
executive_summary:
Cybersecurity Operations Centres (SOCs) — 24/7 facilities monitoring networks for threats and responding to incidents — are widespread in Saudi Arabia, operated by Elm, STC, Cyberani, and government agencies. The gap is threat intelligence: the knowledge of who is attacking, how, and with what tools. Almost all cyber threat intelligence consumed by Saudi SOCs today is English-language threat intelligence from US companies (CrowdStrike, Mandiant, Palo Alto Networks) — with no Arabic threat actor tracking, no Gulf-specific threat landscape analysis, and no understanding of Arabic-language cybercriminal forums.

Node DG-6 targets Saudi Arabic cyber threat intelligence capability: analysts fluent in Arabic monitoring Arabic-language cybercriminal and hacktivist forums, tracking Gulf-specific threat actors, and producing Arabic-language threat intelligence products consumed by Saudi SOCs and government agencies. This combines human intelligence (Arabic-speaking threat analysts) with AI-assisted analysis (Arabic NLP from DG-1).

The global cyber threat intelligence market was valued at $8.2 billion in 2024 growing at 21.3% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's unique exposure to state-sponsored attacks and Gulf regional threat actors creates demand for Saudi-origin threat intelligence that foreign vendors cannot adequately address.

companies.name: Recorded Future
companies.rationale: Recorded Future provides AI-powered threat intelligence and has government partnerships globally. Saudi deployment with Arabic language extension.

companies.name: Flashpoint (Gulf analysts)
companies.rationale: Flashpoint monitors dark web and underground forums. Arabic-language monitoring partnership.

companies.name: Group-IB
companies.rationale: Group-IB has Middle East operations and Arabic-language threat analyst capability.

companies.name: Mandiant (Google)
companies.rationale: Mandiant has state-actor threat intelligence and active Saudi Arabia government engagement.

companies.name: Anomali
companies.rationale: Anomali provides threat intelligence platforms with Arab region deployment capability.

partners.name: NCA (National Cybersecurity Authority)
partners.current: National SOC; government threat sharing; some threat intelligence
partners.upgrade: NCA Arabic Threat Intelligence Centre; 30 Arabic-speaking threat analysts; Recorded Future platform under NCA control; national threat intelligence sharing with all Saudi SOCs
partners.timeline: 18 months to operational Arabic TI centre

partners.name: Cyberani
partners.current: Aramco SOC; OT threat intelligence; some Arabic threat monitoring
partners.upgrade: Commercialise Cyberani Arabic threat intelligence; integrate with NCA national platform; Arabic language dark web monitoring product

partners.name: SDAIA / Arabic NLP (DG-1 connection)
partners.current: Arabic NLP research; national AI programme
partners.upgrade: Arabic NLP from DG-1 Saudi Falcon applied to threat intelligence text analysis; automated Arabic forum monitoring; Arabic malware code analysis

partners.name: KACST Cybersecurity Research
partners.current: Cybersecurity research; some Arabic language security research
partners.upgrade: Arabic threat intelligence research programme; Gulf cybercriminal ecosystem mapping; Saudi-specific threat actor profiling
